parsed_review.summary: CP00-836 is a descriptor-only RP27 Platform Extensibility "failure foundation transition bridge" pack (risk C, 150 units RP27.P06.M10.S11-RP27.P07.M06.S21). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only. Scope is legitimate and ledger-sourced: plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:397) and manifest plan_binding_snapshot both bind CP00-836 to risk C / 150 units / this range with source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65..., matching the binding (registry.js:320) upstream CP00-835, next CP00-837 / RP27.P07.M06.S22. Coverage reconciles exactly: required_section_rows = PERMISSION_ROWS.slice(10,20) for the P06.M10 closeout tail (S11-S20, completing M10 begun in CP00-835) plus FAILURE_ROWS slices for P07 (M00:11, M01:20, M02-M05:22, M06:21, the only split micro-phase). All eight deliverable counts (ui 4, claude_review 6, security_audit 16, test 14, failure_recovery 82, implementation 10, hermes_evidence 12, fixture 6 = 150), phase counts (P06:10, P07:140), micro-phase counts, and micro-title counts match the prompt, manifest, and registry requirements; the manifest/baseline included_units agree row-for-row. Implementation is mutually consistent across the CP836 binding, requirements, case-set builder, Hermes packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false), Claude packet (read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, routes to CP00-837/S22), closeout handoff (runtime_opening_allowed=false, human approval required), descriptor, contract projection, and the contract JSON (current_pack/latest_pack=CP00-836, latest_projection descriptor correct, validation.valid=true with all-false no_write_attestation). The four CP836 model tests are real deterministic assertions over live plan/manifest data (coverage.errors empty, deliverable distribution, descriptor runtime-closed with api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed=false, contract currency). No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold and are hard-enforced: NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and createRow descriptor-only defaults apply to every row, and the descriptor/coverage/contract validators fail on any structural drift, runtime opening, permission/audit write, real-data/secret/permission-detail/audit-body leak, authority-boundary violation, or handoff drift; no real-data or secret patterns were found in the contract JSON. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/evidence handling (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No premature production_ready/enterprise-trust claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, with adjudication/inspection/command-evidence as honest pending-review placeholders; the claude-review-result.json 0-byte stdout is correctly treated as NOT valid (valid_review_receipts=[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, blocks_pack_closeout=true), i.e. CP178+ hardening is functioning, not bypassed, and the readiness auth-preflight passed. Command-evidence records only the two pack-specific commands (rp27:platform-extensibility:validate, platform node --test) at exit 0 with gate_outcome=pending_review; the full closeout validation suite and the final production_ready flip plus human enterprise-trust approval remain outside this receipt by design. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic note only (dead closeoutOverride branch carried over from CP00-835). Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block this pack's closeout nor this CP's goal closeout; no unresolved P0/P1.

findings.title: Unreachable closeout-handoff override branch in CP836 case-set builder
findings.details: createPlatformExtensibilityCp836FailureFoundationTransitionBridgeCaseSet() defines closeoutOverride (registry.js:1941-1944) and applies it only when title === "Closeout handoff" (line 1952). For CP836, required_section_rows are PERMISSION_ROWS.slice(10,20) (Legal hold interaction ... Denied test) and FAILURE_ROWS slices (Failure taxonomy ... Human escalation note); none of these titles equals "Closeout handoff", so the conditional is never taken and closeoutOverride is effectively unused (carried over from the CP00-835 case-set builder, where the same branch was flagged as CP835-P3-01). No functional or security impact: closeout routing is correctly carried by the separate closeout_handoff field via createPlatformExtensibilityCp836CloseoutHandoff() (to_pack_id CP00-837 / next_subphase RP27.P07.M06.S22) and is enforced by the descriptor validator (validators.js:1834). The override values are also correct, so even if reached the branch would be benign.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup: remove the dead closeoutOverride variable and the title === "Closeout handoff" conditional from this builder (and ideally the CP00-835 sibling) to reduce confusion. No change is required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic note. Does not affect the production_ready determination, descriptor correctness, coverage, or the no-write/no-real-data boundaries. Acknowledged as optional future cleanup; CP00-836 closeout is unaffected.
